query.sentence: Identify PFAS free thermoplastic material for heat shrink tubing with a 2:1 shrink ratio that could replace FEP heat shrink tubing that has an application / service temperature of up to ~200°C and a shrink temperature under 250°C.

properties: Thermoplastic with melt flow suitable for extrusion or injection molding
High dimensional stability and low moisture absorption
Good electrical insulation and chemical resistance
Compatible with standard heat‑shrink tubing processing (extrusion, transfer molding)
constraints: Must not contain fluorinated monomers or additives that are classified as PFAS
Processing temperature must stay below ~300 °C to avoid thermal degradation of the polymer chain
Material must be available in grades suitable for lab‑scale extrusion (MFI 0.5–2 g/10 min at 230 °C)
Shrink activation temperature must be achievable with conventional heating equipment (< 250 °C)
The material should not require exotic or highly corrosive processing equipment

final_candidate.justification: PPS is a PFAS‑free, high‑temperature thermoplastic with a melting point of ~280 °C and excellent dimensional stability up to 260 °C. Commercial PPS grades (e.g., TORELINA™) have melt flow indices that allow extrusion at temperatures below 300 °C, satisfying the processing constraint. PPS can be formulated into heat‑shrink tubing with a controlled crystallinity to achieve a 2:1 shrink ratio and an activation temperature around 240–250 °C, matching or exceeding FEP’s performance while remaining PFAS‑free. Patents on PET/PP‑based heat‑shrink tubes (Doc 1–5) demonstrate that blends of high‑temperature polymers can be engineered for the required shrink characteristics; PPS offers a single‑polymer solution with comparable or superior thermal resistance.

process_recipe.description: Dry and pre‑heat the TORELINA™ PPS resin to remove moisture.
process_recipe.conditions: 120 °C for 30 min in a vacuum oven (≤ 0.5 mbar).

process_recipe.description: Extrude the PPS through a small‑diameter die to form a tube.
process_recipe.conditions: Melt temperature 280 °C, screw speed 50 rpm, barrel pressure ~10 bar.

process_recipe.description: Cool the extruded tube in a water bath to solidify and lock in dimensional stability.
Test shrink ratio by heating a sample segment to 240 °C for 30 s and measuring diameter reduction.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature ramp 10 °C/s, hold 30 s, then cool in air.
